list.name: repo
list.label: Repository
list.query: SELECT DISTINCT repo_name FROM energy_jobs ORDER BY repo_name

list.name: job
list.label: Job
list.query: SELECT DISTINCT job_name FROM energy_jobs WHERE repo_name = '$repo' ORDER BY job_name

panels.title: Energy per Commit — Pipeline History
panels.description: Each point = one pipeline run. X=date, Y=total energy, color=branch. Hover to see commit_id.

targets.rawSql: SELECT p.date AS time, p.commit_id, p.branch, p.total_pipeline_energy_j AS energy_j, p.trigger FROM energy_pipelines p WHERE p.repo_name = '$repo' AND p.is_valid = TRUE ORDER BY p.date ASC

panels.title: Job Count per Pipeline over Time
panels.description: Detect when new jobs were added or removed. More jobs = more parallelism or complexity.

targets.rawSql: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('day', date) AS time, COUNT(DISTINCT job_name) AS distinct_jobs, COUNT(*) AS total_runs FROM energy_jobs WHERE repo_name = '$repo' AND is_valid = TRUE GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Energy per Job over Time (Selected Repo)
panels.description: Stacked — see if a specific job started consuming more after a commit

targets.rawSql: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('day', date) AS time, job_name, AVG(total_energy_j) AS avg_j FROM energy_jobs WHERE repo_name = '$repo' AND is_valid = TRUE AND date > NOW()-INTERVAL '90 days' GROUP BY 1, 2 ORDER BY 1, 2

panels.title: Top 20 Commits Ranked by Energy Impact
panels.description: Which commits triggered the most expensive pipelines? Identify regressions.

targets.rawSql: SELECT p.commit_id, p.branch, DATE(p.date) AS run_date, p.trigger, ROUND(p.total_pipeline_energy_j::numeric,2) AS pipeline_j, COUNT(j.id) AS jobs FROM energy_pipelines p LEFT JOIN energy_jobs j ON j.pipeline_id = p.pipeline_id AND j.is_valid = TRUE WHERE p.repo_name = '$repo' AND p.is_valid = TRUE GROUP BY p.commit_id, p.branch, p.date, p.trigger, p.total_pipeline_energy_j ORDER BY pipeline_j DESC LIMIT 20

panels.title: Energy Regression vs Previous Commit
panels.description: Pipeline energy delta vs the previous run on the same branch. Positive = regression.

targets.rawSql: WITH runs AS (SELECT commit_id, branch, date, total_pipeline_energy_j, LAG(total_pipeline_energy_j) OVER (PARTITION BY branch ORDER BY date) AS prev_j FROM energy_pipelines WHERE repo_name = '$repo' AND is_valid = TRUE) SELECT commit_id, branch, ROUND((total_pipeline_energy_j - prev_j)::numeric, 2) AS delta_j, ROUND(100.0*(total_pipeline_energy_j - prev_j)/NULLIF(prev_j,0), 1) AS delta_pct FROM runs WHERE prev_j IS NOT NULL ORDER BY date DESC LIMIT 10

panels.title: CPU vs RAM Energy Evolution (per commit date)
panels.description: Track how component balance changed over time — did GPU start dominating after a new stage?

targets.rawSql: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('day', date) AS time, component, AVG(avg_power_w) AS avg_w FROM energy_granularity WHERE repo_name = '$repo' AND is_valid = TRUE AND date > NOW()-INTERVAL '90 days' GROUP BY 1, 2 ORDER BY 1, 2

panels.title: Avg Job Duration over Time
panels.description: Duration inflation = sign of added complexity or resource starvation

targets.rawSql: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('day', date) AS time, job_name, AVG(duration_s) AS avg_dur_s FROM energy_jobs WHERE repo_name = '$repo' AND is_valid = TRUE AND date > NOW()-INTERVAL '90 days' GROUP BY 1, 2 ORDER BY 1, 2

panels.title: Manual vs Auto Trigger — Energy Impact
panels.description: Manual runs often indicate debugging/testing — are they more expensive?

targets.rawSql: SELECT COALESCE(trigger,'auto') AS trigger_type, SUM(total_energy_j) AS energy_j FROM energy_jobs WHERE repo_name = '$repo' AND is_valid = TRUE GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY energy_j DESC

panels.title: Energy by Branch
panels.description: Feature branches vs main — track wasted energy on failed features

targets.rawSql: SELECT branch, ROUND(SUM(total_energy_j)::numeric,2) AS total_j, COUNT(*) AS runs FROM energy_jobs WHERE repo_name = '$repo' AND is_valid = TRUE GROUP BY branch ORDER BY total_j DESC LIMIT 10

panels.title: Zero-Energy Suspect Runs over Time
panels.description: Runs with duration>10s but total_energy=0 — likely measurement failures

targets.rawSql: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('day', date) AS time, COUNT(*) AS suspect_count FROM energy_jobs WHERE repo_name = '$repo' AND total_energy_j = 0 AND duration_s > 10 AND date > NOW()-INTERVAL '90 days' GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1
